head.hi: Sir M. H. Herbert to Mr. Foster.

div.p: Dear Mr. Foster: With reference to my note to Mr. Hay of the 29th May, I received to-day from the British agent the inclosed detailed list of the documents the production of which he has already asked for.

frus:attachment.head: DIPLOMATIC CORRESPONDENCE RELATING TO THE TREATY OF 1825 BETWEEN GREAT BRITAIN AND RUSSIA.
frus:attachment.p:
Originals of the following letters and documents:
Date.
U. S. Appendix.
1822. Nov. 2. Tuyll to Nesselrode 112
Oct. 21.
1823. Nov. 3. Poletica to Nesselrode 132–7
1824. May 20. Lieven to Nesselrode 177–9
June 1.
1824. May 21. “ “ “ 179
June 2.
1824. July 13. “ “ “ 185–6
July 25.
Explanations with regard to contre projet 205–6
1824. Sept. 19. Lieven to Nesselrode 207
Oct. 1.
1825. Feb. 1. Contre projet submitted by S. Canning 213–17
Feb. 13.
S. Canning’s contre projet as altered and corrected by Matusevich 218–23
1825. Feb. 20. Nesselrode to Lieven 225
Mar. 3.
1825. May 8. Lieven to Nesselrode 229–31
May 20.
152–3
1824. Feb. 20. Admiral Mordvinof to Nesselrode 152–3
1824. Apr. 11. Nesselrode to Mordvinof 166–9
1825. Feb. 17. Middleton to Adams 224–5
Mar. 1.

p.hi: The originals of the following papers relating to the Russian occupation up to the cession to the United States in 1867, and set out in the Appendix to the United States case, pp. 251–323.

row.cell: Governor Simpson to the manager of the Russian-American Company
Hudsons Bay Company to Russian-American Company
Director Severin to the minister of finance
Report of the governor to the board of directors of the Russian-American Company
Report of the governor of the board of directors of the Russian-American Company
The governor of the Russian-American Company to the commander of the schooner Chilkat, Second Lieutenant Kuznetsoff
Baron Wrangell to board of directors of the Russian-American Company
Report of the directors of the Russian-American Company on the Dryad affair
Board of directors of the Russian-American Company to the department of trade and manufactures
The board of directors of the Russian-American Company to the governor of the Russian-American colonies, Ivan Antonovich Kupreyanoff
The governor of the Russian colonies in America to the commander of the brig Chichagoff, Lieutenant
Report of the governor of the Russian colonies in America to the board of directors
Report of the governor to the general board of the Russian-American Company
Journal of correspondence, 1839, No. 3, kept during the voyage in the Straits and the tour of inspection to Redoubt St. Dionysius, from June 6, 1839, to June 23, 1839
Report of the board of directors of the Russian-American Company
Report of governor of Russian colonies in America to the board of directors of the Russian-American Company
Translation of the Russian memorandum marked A. A. By S. N. Buynitsky

p.hi: The originals of the following papers relating to United States occupation after the cession, copies of which or extracts from which, are set out in the Appendix to the United States case, pp. 345–510.

row.cell: Instructions to Lieut. Col. Robert N. Scott
Colonel Scott’s report on the Indians, 1867, with inclosures
Report of Maj. Gen. George H. Thomas
Acting Secretary of the Navy to the Secretary of State
Richard W. Meade, jr., commanding U. S. S. Saginaw, to Rear-Admiral T. T. Craven, commanding North Pacific Squadron
Richard W. Meade, jr., commanding U. S. S. Saginaw, to Rear-Admiral T. T. Craven, commanding North Pacific Squadron
A. M. Pennock, rear-admiral commanding U. S. Naval Force on North Pacific Station, to Hon. George M. Robeson, Secretary of the Navy, and note A
A. M. Pennock, rear-admiral commanding U. S. Naval Force on North Pacific Station, to Hon. George M. Robeson, Secretary of the Navy, and note A
Capt. L. A. Beardslee to the Secretary of the Navy and inclosures
Commander Glass to the Secretary of the Navy, with inclosures
The Secretary of the Navy to Commander Glass
Commander Glass to the Secretary of the Navy
Commander Glass to the Secretary of the Navy, with report of G. C. Hanus
Commander Lull to the Secretary of the Navy
Commander Coghlan to the Secretary of the Navy
Commander Nicholls to the Secretary of the Navy and a
Commander Nicholls to the officer of detail and note b
Mr. Hahn and others to Governor Swineford
Lieutenant-Commander Newell to the Secretary of the Navy
Lieutenant-Commander Newell to the Secretary of the Navy
Governor Swineford to Lieutenant-Commander Newell, inclosing letter of Max Pracht
Lieutenant-Commander Newell to the Secretary of the Navy
Lieutenant-Commander Newell to the Secretary of the Navy, inclosing report of Lieutenant McCrackin’s trip over Chilcoot trail, with Exhibits A and B

p.hi: The original logs of United States naval vessels on duty in Alaskan waters, from which the following extracts have been taken:

row.cell: Log of the United States flagship Saranac, second rate, 13 guns, under the command of Capt. Thomas S. Phelps, U. S. Navy
Log of the U. S. S. Wachusetts, third rate, 7 guns, under the command of Edward P. Lull, U. S. Navy
Log of the U. S. S. Adams, third rate, 6 guns, under the command of Commander J. B. Coghlan, U. S. Navy
Logs of the U. S. S. Pinta, fourth rate, 2 guns, under the command of Lieutenant-Commander H. E. Nichols, U. S. Navy
Logs of the U. S. S. Pinta, fourth rate, 2 guns, under command of Lieutenant J. S. Newell, U. S. Navy
Log of the U. S. S. Pinta, fourth rate, under the command of Lieutenant-Commander O. W. Farenholt, U. S. Navy

p.hi: The originals of the following letters from naval officers:

row.cell: H. M. Dombaugh, lieutenant-commander, U. S. Navy, to the Chief of Bureau of Navigation
John W. Stewart, lieutenant, U. S. Navy, to Chief of Bureau of Navigation
Admiral J. B. Coghlan to Secretary of the Navy
G. T. Emmons, lieutenant, U. S. Navy, retired, to the Secretary of the Navy
H. C. Taylor, rear-admiral, U. S. Navy, to the Secretary of the Navy

frus:attachment.p: Exercise of Jurisdiction in Alaska by Judicial Authorities.

p.hi: The original of the following letter:

row.cell: The Attorney-General to the Secretary of State

p.hi: The original court record and other original papers or books referring to the following cases alleged to have been tried before U. S. tribunals in Alaska:
The originals of the following letters and depositions:

row.cell: Deposition of J. Bernard Moore, and notice
Letters, memorandum, and appointment certified to by W. J. Hills, by J. J. Clarke
Deposition of Lewis L. Williams, and attached papers marked A, B, C, D, and E

p.hi: Enforcement of Revenue Laws and Exercise of Authority by Treasury Officials.
The originals of the following letters, reports, and documents:

row.cell: The Acting Secretary of the Treasury to the Secretary of State
The Secretary of the Treasury to the Secretary of State
Memorandum relating to the establishment and maintenance of the United States customs service in southeastern Alaska, as shown by the records of the Treasury Department
Report from Acting Collector Dodge, and accompanying documents
Extract from letter of Collector Ketchum to the Secretary of the Treasury
Report of Special Agent W. G. Morris, of March 12, 1880, and of the collector at Sitka, March 27, 1880, and the Department’s letter of April 2, 1880
Letter, H. F. French to collector of customs, Sitka
Letters of January 1 and February 19, 1881, to the collector at Sitka, and the latter’s reports of November 5, 1881, and August 9, 1882
Reports of July 12, 1890, from collector at Sitka, and Department’s letter of August 19, 1890, to that officer
Letter, John J. Healy to the governor of Alaska
Collector at Sitka to Secretary of Treasury
Acting Secretary to collector at Sitka
Collector at Sitka to Secretary of Treasury
Extracts from report of William Gouverneur Morris, special Treasury agent, Alaska, to the Secretary of the Treasury
Reports of officers of the United States Revenue Marine
Letters from officers of the Revenue-Cutter Service

p.hi: The originals of the following reports, relating to schools, census, and control of the Indians, and mining locations on the mainland:

row.cell: The Secretary of the Interior to the Secretary of State
The Secretary of the Interior to the Secretary of State
The Commissioner of Education to the Secretary of the Interior
Rev. Sheldon Jackson to the Secretary of State
Extracts from reports of the governors of Alaska
Report of the Rev. Sheldon Jackson, D. D., upon the condition of education in Alaska, 1881
Census of Alaska Indians, 1880. Census of Alaska
Indians, 1890. Statement from the Commissioner of the General Land Office
The originals of the entries referred to in abstract of mineral locations in the vicinity of Lynn Canal, 1885 to 1895
Letter, Postmaster-General to the Secretary of State with inclosed list of post-offices

p.hi: Originals of the following miscellaneous letters and documents:

row.cell: Mr. Titmann to the Secretary of State
Mr. Titmann to the Secretary of State, and inclosed petition

Mr. Titmann to the Secretary of State, inclosing affidavit of Lindenkole, and letters exchanged in 1867 with Hon. W. H. Seward, Secretary of State, and the assistant in charge of the Coast Survey, respecting the map of Northwestern America, compiled for Department of State at the Coast Survey Office in 1867

frus:attachment.p: Testimony Secured in the Year 1899 among the Chilkat Indians.

row.cell: Letter of John Tweedale, major, U. S. Army, etc., to Secretary of State
